name: Conference Signup System
description: A registration system where conference attendees sign up via a browser-based portal backed by a REST service and a database. The service and database are deployed in a Kubernetes cluster.

encryption-in-transit.description: All data transmitted between system components must be encrypted using TLS 1.3 or higher with validated certificates. This control applies system-wide to every connection in this architecture.

nodes.name: Conference Attendee
nodes.description: Person registering for the conference via the signup portal.

nodes.name: Conference Website
nodes.description: Browser-based signup portal where attendees complete and submit their registration details.

nodes.name: Load Balancer
nodes.description: Routes incoming HTTPS traffic from the conference website to the attendees service. Terminates TLS from the public internet.

nodes.name: Attendees Service
nodes.description: Backend service handling signup requests: validates input, persists registrations, and returns confirmation responses.

nodes.name: Attendees Store
nodes.description: Persistent storage for registered attendees. Holds registration records including name, email, and session preferences.

nodes.name: Kubernetes Cluster
nodes.description: Production Kubernetes deployment environment hosting the load balancer, attendees service, and attendees store.

relationships.description: Conference attendee uses the conference website to complete and submit the signup form.
Conference website sends signup requests to the load balancer over HTTPS.
Load balancer forwards requests to the attendees service using mutual TLS for service-to-service authentication within the cluster.
Attendees service reads and writes registration records to the attendees store via JDBC.
The load balancer, attendees service, and attendees store are deployed-in the Kubernetes cluster. The k8s-cluster node is the runtime container for these three components.
